topic: ChatGPT Super App Review 2026: Unified AI Platform with Codex, Atlas, and GPT-6

key_points: ChatGPT 5.5 and unified super app launched April 6, 2026
Merges ChatGPT, Codex, and Atlas browser into one platform
Focuses on memory and task continuity improvements as bridge to GPT-6
Context-aware unified workspace for chatting, coding, browsing

Deep analysis of how ChatGPT acts as conversational and reasoning core
Covers the OS-level ambitions of OpenAI's unified platform
Comprehensive how-to guide for new super app users
Step-by-step walkthrough of features and use cases

stats.fact: ChatGPT weekly active users reached 900 million as of February 2026, more than double the 400 million in February 2025
ChatGPT monthly active users estimated between 1.2 and 1.5 billion including all integrations
OpenAI generates $2 billion per month in revenue with $25 billion annualized revenue as of early 2026
ChatGPT holds 60-80% of the global AI chatbot market as of early 2026
ChatGPT enterprise seats exceeded 7 million, up approximately 9x year over year
OpenAI Codex scores 7.5/10 for coding automation, excelling at single-task code generation (8.5/10)
GPT-6 (codename Spud) expected to launch between April-June 2026; prediction markets assign ~90% probability by June 30
Atlas browser is currently macOS-only and in beta since March 2026; Windows and mobile versions not yet released
ChatGPT Plus plan at $20/mo includes full model suite, Deep Research (10 runs/mo), Sora, Codex, and Agent Mode

angles: Is the ChatGPT super app a genuine productivity revolution or a walled garden lock-in strategy?
